tools.name: caplia_search
tools.description: Search Caplia for companies in the caller's pipeline by name. Returns up to 25 matches with id, name, website, industry, and stage. Use this to find a company before fetching its full profile.

tools.name: caplia_list_companies
tools.description: List companies in the caller's pipeline. Supports cursor pagination (`cursor` from the previous response's `next_cursor`) and filtering by `stage` or by named `view`. Use `limit` (default 25, max 100) to bound the page size.

tools.name: caplia_get_company
tools.description: Fetch the full profile of one company by UUID: name, problem statement, industry, stage, website, founders, fundraising round, etc.

tools.name: caplia_get_company_scores
tools.description: Get the CRI score and per-thesis match scores for a company. Returns `{ cri: { score, scored_at, domains }, thesis_matches: [...] }`. Useful when an agent needs to reason about how a company fits the team's investment theses.

tools.name: caplia_get_company_metrics
tools.description: Get traction and key metrics for a company: revenue, growth, headcount, fundraising round milestones, etc.

tools.name: caplia_list_company_documents
tools.description: List all data-room documents attached to a company: pitch decks, financial models, founder updates, term sheets. Each entry has id, name, folder, type, size, and created_at.

tools.name: caplia_get_document_url
tools.description: Get a 1-hour signed download URL for a specific data-room document. The agent can fetch the bytes directly from the returned URL — file content does not pass through the API.

tools.name: caplia_list_theses
tools.description: List the team's active investment theses with their descriptions. Useful for an agent that's reasoning about whether a deal fits a thesis.

tools.name: caplia_list_properties
tools.description: List the team's custom properties (e.g. an awards programme's "Award Category") with their selectable options. Properties are configured per team inside Caplia, so call this before caplia_submit_deck when you want to tag a submission — pass selections via its `properties` argument using the option i

tools.name: caplia_list_views
tools.description: List the team's configured pipeline views (e.g. Deal Flow, My Pipeline, Screening). Each view has a key that can be used as the `view` argument on caplia_list_companies.

tools.name: caplia_get_view_companies
tools.description: Fetch the companies belonging to a named pipeline view (e.g. `deal-flow`, `my-pipeline`).

tools.name: caplia_get_job
tools.description: Poll the status of an async job (typically the result of a deck submission). Returns `{ status, company_id, results, errors }`. Recommended polling cadence: every 3 seconds.

tools.name: caplia_submit_deck
tools.description: Submit a pitch deck (PDF) to the caller's Caplia pipeline. The deck flows through the same intake pipeline as web uploads and email forwards: text extraction → company shell creation → CRI scoring → thesis matching. Returns `{ job_id, status: "queued", poll_url }` immediately; use `caplia_get_job` w

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp.venture.caplia.ai" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: caplia_get_document_url · Private data: caplia_list_company_documents, caplia_get_document_url · Public sink: caplia_submit_deck.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: caplia_get_document_url · Private data: caplia_list_company_documents, caplia_get_document_url · Public sink: caplia_submit_deck

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
